documentation.value:
Outputs an error message to the web console.

```js
console.error('Something went wrong:', err);
```

Outputs a warning message to the web console.

```js
console.warn('Deprecation notice:', msg);
```

Outputs an informational message to the console.

```js
console.info('Server started on port', port);
```

Displays tabular data as a table in the console.

```js
console.table([{ name: 'Alice', age: 30 }, { name: 'Bob', age: 25 }]);
```

Displays an interactive listing of the properties of a specified JavaScript object.

```js
console.dir(document.body, { depth: 2 });
```

Stops a timer that was previously started by calling `console.time()`.

```js
console.timeEnd('myTimer');
```

Log a message and stack trace if the first argument is falsy.

```js
console.assert(x > 0, 'x must be positive');
```

Parses a JSON string and returns the JavaScript value or object described by the string.

```js
const obj = JSON.parse('{"name":"Alice"}');
```

**Throws** `SyntaxError` if the string is not valid JSON.

Converts a JavaScript value to a JSON string.

```js
const json = JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2);
```

**Parameters:**
- `value` — The value to convert
- `replacer` — A function or array of strings to filter properties
- `space` — Number of spaces for indentation

Creates a new, shallow-copied Array instance from an iterable or array-like object.

```js
Array.from('hello');        // ['h','e','l','l','o']
Array.from({length: 3}, (_, i) => i); // [0, 1, 2]
```

completions.label: Array.isArray
completions.detail: Check if value is an array

documentation.value:
Determines whether the passed value is an Array.

```js
Array.isArray([1, 2, 3]); // true
Array.isArray('string');  // false
```

Creates a new array with the results of calling a function on every element.

```js
const doubled = [1,2,3].map(x => x * 2); // [2,4,6]
```

Creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

```js
const evens = [1,2,3,4].filter(x => x % 2 === 0); // [2,4]
```

Executes a reducer function on each element, resulting in a single output value.

```js
const sum = [1,2,3].reduce((acc, val) => acc + val, 0); // 6
```

Executes a provided function once for each array element.

```js
[1,2,3].forEach(item => console.log(item));
```

Returns the first element in the array that satisfies the provided testing function.

```js
const found = [1,5,10].find(x => x > 3); // 5
```

Returns the index of the first element that satisfies the provided testing function. Returns -1 if not found.

```js
const idx = [1,5,10].findIndex(x => x > 3); // 1
```

Tests whether at least one element passes the provided function.

```js
[1,2,3].some(x => x > 2); // true
```

Tests whether all elements pass the provided function.

```js
[2,4,6].every(x => x % 2 === 0); // true
```

Determines whether an array includes a certain value.

```js
[1,2,3].includes(2); // true
```

Creates a new array with all sub-array elements concatenated into it recursively up to the specified depth.

```js
[1, [2, [3]]].flat(Infinity); // [1, 2, 3]
```

Maps each element using a mapping function, then flattens the result into a new array.

```js
[1,2,3].flatMap(x => [x, x*2]); // [1,2,2,4,3,6]
```

Sorts the elements of an array in place and returns the sorted array.

```js
[3,1,2].sort((a, b) => a - b); // [1,2,3]
```

Changes the contents of an array by removing or replacing existing elements and/or adding new elements in place.

```js
const arr = [1,2,3,4];
arr.splice(1, 2); // removes 2 elements starting at index 1
```

Returns a shallow copy of a portion of an array.

```js
[1,2,3,4].slice(1, 3); // [2, 3]
```

Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable property names.

```js
Object.keys({ a: 1, b: 2 }); // ['a', 'b']
```

Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable property values.

```js
Object.values({ a: 1, b: 2 }); // [1, 2]
```

Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable [key, value] pairs.

```js
Object.entries({ a: 1, b: 2 }); // [['a',1], ['b',2]]
```

Copies all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object.

```js
const merged = Object.assign({}, defaults, options);
```

Freezes an object. A frozen object can no longer be changed.

```js
const config = Object.freeze({ port: 3000, host: 'localhost' });
```

Takes an iterable of promises and returns a single Promise that resolves when all have resolved.

```js
const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()]);
```

completions.label: Promise.allSettled
completions.detail: Wait for all promises to settle

documentation.value:
Returns a promise that resolves after all given promises have either fulfilled or rejected.

```js
const results = await Promise.allSettled([p1, p2, p3]);
results.forEach(r => console.log(r.status)); // 'fulfilled' or 'rejected'
```

Returns a promise that fulfills or rejects as soon as one of the promises settles.

```js
const result = await Promise.race([fetch(url), timeout(5000)]);
```

Takes an iterable of promises and returns the first one that fulfills. Only rejects if all reject.

```js
const fastest = await Promise.any([mirror1(), mirror2()]);
```

Parses a string argument and returns an integer of the specified radix.

```js
parseInt('42');     // 42
parseInt('0xFF', 16); // 255
```

Parses a string and returns a floating point number.

```js
parseFloat('3.14'); // 3.14
```

Determines whether the passed value is NaN (more reliable than global `isNaN`).

```js
Number.isNaN(NaN);        // true
Number.isNaN(undefined);  // false
```

Determines whether the passed value is a finite number.

```js
Number.isFinite(42);       // true
Number.isFinite(Infinity); // false
```

Returns the largest of zero or more numbers.

```js
Math.max(1, 5, 3);       // 5
Math.max(...[1, 5, 3]);  // 5
```

Returns the smallest of zero or more numbers.

```js
Math.min(1, 5, 3);       // 1
Math.min(...[1, 5, 3]);  // 1
```

Returns the largest integer less than or equal to a given number.

```js
Math.floor(4.7);  // 4
Math.floor(-4.1); // -5
```

Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to a given number.

```js
Math.ceil(4.1); // 5
```

Returns the value of a number rounded to the nearest integer.

```js
Math.round(4.5); // 5
Math.round(4.4); // 4
```

Returns a pseudo-random number between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive).

```js
const rand = Math.random();
const randInt = Math.floor(Math.random() * 10); // 0-9
```

The `Map` object holds key-value pairs and remembers the original insertion order.

```js
const map = new Map();
map.set('key', 'value');
map.get('key'); // 'value'
map.has('key'); // true
map.size;       // 1
```

The `Set` object lets you store unique values of any type.

```js
const set = new Set([1, 2, 3, 2]);
set.size;       // 3
set.has(2);     // true
set.add(4);
```

Creates a deep clone of a given value using the structured clone algorithm.

```js
const clone = structuredClone({ nested: { data: [1, 2] } });
```

The `globalThis` property provides a standard way of accessing the global `this` value across environments.

```js
globalThis.myGlobal = 42;
```

Extract properties from objects into variables.

```js
const { name, age, ...rest } = person;
```

Extract elements from arrays into variables.

```js
const [first, second, ...rest] = [1, 2, 3, 4];
```

Allows embedded expressions and multi-line strings.

```js
const msg = `Hello, ${name}! You are ${age} years old.`;
```

Returns the right-hand operand when the left-hand is null or undefined.

```js
const value = input ?? 'default';
```

Returns a new string with all matches of a pattern replaced.

```js
'aaa'.replaceAll('a', 'b'); // 'bbb'
```

Returns the element at the given index, supporting negative indexing.

```js
[1,2,3].at(-1); // 3
'hello'.at(0);   // 'h'
```

Creates and returns a URL object from a URL string.

```js
const url = new URL('https://example.com/path?q=1');
url.hostname;    // 'example.com'
url.searchParams.get('q'); // '1'
```

Utility methods to work with the query string of a URL.

```js
const params = new URLSearchParams({ page: 1, limit: 10 });
params.toString(); // 'page=1&limit=10'
```

Decodes a string of data which has been encoded using Base64 encoding.

```js
atob('SGVsbG8='); // 'Hello'
```

Creates a Base64-encoded ASCII string from a binary string.

```js
btoa('Hello'); // 'SGVsbG8='
```

Returns a string indicating the type of the operand.

```js
typeof 42;          // 'number'
typeof 'hello';     // 'string'
typeof undefined;   // 'undefined'
typeof null;        // 'object' (historical bug)
```

Tests whether the prototype property of a constructor appears in the prototype chain.

```js
[] instanceof Array;  // true
new Date() instanceof Date; // true
```

Creates an error object.

```js
throw new Error('Something went wrong');
```

Built-in error types: `TypeError`, `RangeError`, `SyntaxError`, `ReferenceError`, `URIError`, `EvalError`

Enables language-sensitive number formatting.

```js
new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', {
  style: 'currency',
  currency: 'USD'
}).format(1234.56); // '$1,234.56'
```

Creates an Iterator object from an iterable or iterator-like object.

```js
const it = Iterator.from([1, 2, 3]);
it.map(x => x * 2).toArray(); // [2, 4, 6]
```

Creates a new Array from an async iterable, iterable, or array-like object.

```js
const arr = await Array.fromAsync(asyncGenerator());
```

Insert a JSX element. ```jsx
Content
```

Declare component state with `useState`.

```jsx
const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
```
